verbGlossary.CONTAINS: A parent {target} fully encloses a child location within its geographic or structural boundary; every record attached to the child also belongs to the parent
verbGlossary.BORDERS: {target} sits at or beyond the property line of the project site, requiring easement or permit access rather than direct containment
verbGlossary.DIVIDES INTO: {target} or structure is vertically segmented into discrete levels (floors, below-grade, roof) that form the primary vertical organizing hierarchy
verbGlossary.SUBDIVIDES INTO: {target} is horizontally partitioned into smaller named areas for work organization, scope separation, or occupancy definition
verbGlossary.STACKS BELOW: A below-grade {target} sits directly beneath an above-grade floor level, sharing structural slab and requiring coordination of waterproofing, MEP risers, and vertical access
verbGlossary.BOUNDED BY: {target} is physically defined or enclosed by a surface, plenum, or assembly that forms one of its boundaries (walls, ceiling plane, deck above)
verbGlossary.PENETRATED BY: A vertical element such as a core, {target}, or opening passes through a horizontal location, creating fire-rated separations that must be maintained at every floor
verbGlossary.HOUSES: {target}, functional area, or space contains a specific piece of installed equipment identified by a unique asset tag for commissioning and operations tracking
verbGlossary.PIERCED BY: A rated assembly or enclosure has a penetration or opening through it that requires firestopping, sealing, or inspection for code compliance
verbGlossary.SPANS THROUGH: A vertical element (core, {target}, stair) extends continuously through multiple floor levels, requiring coordinated work sequencing and fire-rated separation at each level
verbGlossary.ENCLOSES: A regulatory compartment boundary (fire or smoke) wraps around one or more {target}s or spaces, defining where rated construction and life-safety devices are required
verbGlossary.REGULATED AT: {source}inspection, firestopping, or device requirements at each penetration or opening that crosses it
verbGlossary.OVERLAYS: A temporary work {target} or phase area is superimposed on a permanent location (floor, zone) to define where active construction, safety restrictions, or ICRA barriers apply at a given time
verbGlossary.GROUPS: A commissioning {target} aggregates multiple floors, spaces, or equipment locations into a single functional testing boundary organized by mechanical system rather than physical hierarchy
verbGlossary.REFERENCES: Structural {target} line intersections provide the universal coordinate system used to locate elements, columns, and equipment positions at any floor level within the building
verbGlossary.LOCATES: {target} line intersection pinpoints the exact plan position of a major equipment piece, column, or building element for survey layout and field verification
verbGlossary.SURROUNDS: {target} wraps around the outside of a building, organized by facade orientation or function, where enclosure, site work, and landscaping occur
verbGlossary.SERVES: A laydown or {target} provides material storage and equipment staging that supports construction activities at an adjacent building or structure
verbGlossary.MEASURED AT: {source}is calculated at {target} level (floor, room) to identify where problems concentrate
verbGlossary.COUNTED IN: {source}tallies individual deficiency items within {target}-level location for closeout tracking and cross-project benchmarking
verbGlossary.TRACKED ON: {source}rate metric monitors the percentage of items closed at {target} over time to measure vertical closeout progress
verbGlossary.ASSESSED ACROSS: A coverage metric evaluates inspection thoroughness across all {target}s within a building to identify uninspected areas
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO: {source}is geographically associated with a specific work {target} or phase area to identify high-risk areas on site
verbGlossary.INSPECTED AT: A firestopping deficiency rate is measured at individual penetrations or {target}s where rated assembly integrity must be verified
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED FROM: {target} depth utilization is measured starting from the project site root, counting how many hierarchy levels down a company attaches field records
